methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $129M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
INERATEC is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Tim Boeltken that has raised $129M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 9 comparable companies—1 public (LNZA) and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage. The private peer universe includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc., among others.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $129M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $245M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $355M, with the peer range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The wide upper tail reflects outliers like Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $208M (low) to $408M (high), with a median point of $300M. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively; peer-median sentiment runs higher. Key uncertainty stems from the heterogeneity within the climate tech peer set—valuations vary by over 40x—making stage-relative positioning and technology differentiation material to valuation placement within the range.
